title: Authentication, Resource Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Reader Privacy · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for authentication, resource authorization, tenant isolation, and reader privacy and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for privacy-minimized tenant authorization system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.
Authorize application commands from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, purpose, and current state rather than route alone.
Require tenant scope in repository methods, SQL, keys, constraints, caches, tests, metrics, exports, support, and admin operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Token Verification · changed case · Quiz Feed Ts Token Veri…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 7347 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove token verification, resource policy, tenant repositories, data minimization, and audit redaction; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
